action.reasoning: Observation: Baseline test EER=0.1181 (dev EER~0.0004) with wav2vec2-base + mean-pool + linear head after 5 epochs; rapid loss drop to ~0.003 indicates heavy overfitting to seen attacks (A01-A06). Model print confirms 94M params, simple architecture. WavLM-large is pre-cached. Analysis: Large generalization gap due to weak backbone/head for OOD test (unseen attacks A07+, telephony codecs, disjoint speakers). Stats pooling (mean+std) + MLP with BN/dropout + WavLM-large (317M) is standard for ASVspoof LA and stays under 1B params. History shows max_length+bf16+small bs prevents OOM. Decision: Rewrite model.py with WavLMModel+StatsPool+MLP; update train.py (pretrained='microsoft/wavlm-large', bs_train=8/bs_dev=32/epochs=8, add weight_decay) and predict.py (same pretrained, bs=64) for compatibility. Run training to save best.pt on dev EER, then submit_eval immediately per workflow for new baseline and time measurement (~expected 40min).
